text:
Once upon a time in a quiet town nestled between mountains and forests, there lived a girl named Lily. She loved collecting feathers. It wasn't just beautiful things—they whispered stories. Her favorite was a tiny mourning dove grey with a single glossy black feather.

Lily lived in a world where animals spoke. They fluttered about her garden singing the old songs of birds. One autumn day, as she sat picking feathers from fallen branches, her pet owl Hootsie swooped down.

"Good morningya!" sang Hootsie.

 *"Just picking a few.* *"You always steal mine."* She threw one feather.

*"Nothin'.**

She went back indoors only to hear mysterious tapping.

 *"Who?"*

Just then her favorite pet squirrel popped his head out.

 *"You know. Hawthorne's ghost."*

She laughed.

That night a big storm. Lightning hit a old tree next door. Out fell feathers all over town!

 *"Oh dear.** She ran outside.

 *"
